counters.description: summary of threats: azir struggles vs. early game champions who either outrange him (artillery mages) or have many ways to get on top of him (assassins and some bruisers). his ideal matchups are against melee and short-range champions with either low mobility, low cc, and/or similarly weak early games (eg. annie, yasuo, vladimir), especially if they rely on hitting you in lane to scale (e.g. veigar, swain). [note: this section is being developed. now that i have completed the base guide, i am working on giving a highly detailed game plan for each matchup. some of them are complete.]

at the moment (25.4), mel is simply op vs. other ranged champions. she is a lane bully (think hwei on steroids) but unlike hwei has an exploitable weakness. you win with smart wave management and macro.  you also generally win the early 2v2. [i anticipate this ranking to fall pretty low as she receives more nerfs.]

this matchup is extremely annoying. he has superior range, superior burst, superior mana sustain, and superior cc. his only weakness is mobility, but he can avoid jungle ganks by playing from fountain. very strong laning phase, lowish mobility. you win by scaling.
you win if she doesn't snowball. very strong dueling,  sustain, mobility, and roaming.  weak river skirmishes. shut down by wave management and ganks.

nasus mid is one of the worst things to come from the mid-lane map changes.  the lane is shorter with less gank routes, so it is harder to punish him for stacking q. you win by getting priority for your team in the early game.
you win if you can delay his power spikes with frequent jungle ganks. very strong laning phase, no mobility.
you win if she can't snowball. strong laning phase, mobility, roams, and gank setup. very squishy.
you win if you can delay her power spikes with frequent jungle ganks. very strong laning phase, no mobility.
you win if she doesn't snowball. weak levels 1-2, strong mobility and river skirmishes. this matchup is very micro-intensive.

[jng] if the enemy has a nocturne on their team, you always have to be thinking about r. if he catches you with your e on cooldown, you are dead. you cannot ult him away as he will wait to use his spell shield until he sees the animation. zhonya's hourglass is a good early buy if you are struggling.

[jng] if the enemy has a hecarim on their team, you have to be very careful about showing on vision with low health, especially without e.  you cannot ult him out of his r, and once you are feared it is too late. he can chase you down no matter where you are (turret or no turret), so if you do have e, your best option is to escape into the jungle (out of vision). if the hecarim is fed, rylai's crystal scepter is a good buy.

synergies.description: summary of synergies: azir synergizes best with strong frontline champions, especially those with strong peel/engage. he also pairs very well with strong ad ganking junglers that can help carry him through his weak early game.
this is my favorite azir pairing.  ① if you ult a champion toward poppy, she can e them into your wall to stun them.
① azir's soldiers stack sejuani's passive stun. each additional soldier adds an extra stack.
① azir's soldiers stack braum's passive stun. each additional soldier attacking a target adds an extra stack.
① azir's main win condition is to out-scale his opponents through items. ornn's masterwork item further bolsters your late game.
① amumu's passive increases ap damage from allies.
azir should be the only ap champion on your team as his dps is unmatched. having additional squishy ap champions makes your team more vulnerable.
azir should be the only ap champion on your team as his dps is unmatched. having additional squishy ap champions makes your team more vulnerable.
azir should be the only ap champion on your team as his dps is unmatched. having extra, squishy ap champions makes your team more vulnerable. {example: karthus}
① after shuffling one or many champions, mega gnar can ult them into your wall to stun them.
strong ad ganking junglers can help carry azir through his weak early game. bonus points if they have a large aoe ultimate to compliment your shuffles. {examples: wukong, volibear, jarvan iv}
strong ad ganking junglers can help carry azir through his weak early game. bonus points if they have a large aoe ultimate to compliment your shuffles. {examples: wukong, volibear, jarvan iv}
strong ad ganking junglers can help carry azir through his weak early game. bonus points if they have a large aoe ultimate to compliment your shuffles. {examples: wukong, volibear, jarvan iv}
① vayne can e an enemy into your wall to stun them.
① yasuo can ult champions that you knock up with your shuffle.

azir can benefit slightly from enchanter supports that offer attack speed or on-hit affects. however, as a control mage he generally does not want to be as close to the enemy as these enchanters typically do, he does not have enough bulk to protect them, and the con of not having an engage/tank in the support slot outweighs the positives. {examples: nami and renata glasc}

azir can benefit slightly from enchanter supports that offer attack speed or on-hit affects. however, as a control mage he generally does not want to be as close to the enemy as these enchanters typically do, he does not have enough bulk to protect them, and the con of not having an engage/tank in the support slot outweighs the positives.
